messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetAccountInventoryRequest
messages.description:
AC2EvidenceServiceGetAccountInventoryRequest fetches a page of the
account inventory. `as_of` enables point-in-time evidence.

fields.name: kind_filter
fields.description: kind_filter narrows the report. Empty = all kinds.
fields.label: optional

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetAccountInventoryResponse
messages.description: AC2EvidenceServiceGetAccountInventoryResponse returns a page of inventory rows.

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetDormantAccountsRequest
messages.description:
AC2EvidenceServiceGetDormantAccountsRequest fetches a page of dormant
accounts. `dormancy_threshold_days` overrides tenant policy for ad-hoc
reports.

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetDormantAccountsResponse
messages.description:
AC2EvidenceServiceGetDormantAccountsResponse returns a page of dormant
account rows.

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetLifecycleDecisionsRequest
messages.description:
AC2EvidenceServiceGetLifecycleDecisionsRequest fetches a page of audit
rows whose `action` matches one of `action_prefix` over [start_at, end_at].

fields.name: action_prefix
fields.description:
Filter by action prefix(es), e.g. "identity.account." or
"identity.recertification.". Empty = all actions.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetLifecycleDecisionsResponse
messages.description:
AC2EvidenceServiceGetLifecycleDecisionsResponse returns a page of lifecycle
decision rows.

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetRecertificationStatusRequest
messages.description:
AC2EvidenceServiceGetRecertificationStatusRequest fetches a page of
recertification posture. `only_overdue=true` returns just past-due
accounts.

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetRecertificationStatusResponse
messages.description:
AC2EvidenceServiceGetRecertificationStatusResponse returns a page of
recertification posture rows.

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetRoleBindingsRequest
messages.description: AC2EvidenceServiceGetRoleBindingsRequest fetches a page of role bindings.

messages.name: AC2EvidenceServiceGetRoleBindingsResponse
messages.description: AC2EvidenceServiceGetRoleBindingsResponse returns a page of binding rows.

messages.name: AccountInventoryRow
messages.description:
AccountInventoryRow is one row of the account inventory (AC-2(a)/(b)).
`kind` values: "human" | "service" | "workload" | "admin".
`owner_account_id` is set for service accounts and empty for humans.

messages.name: DormantAccountRow
messages.description: DormantAccountRow is one row of the dormant-account report (AC-2(3)).

messages.name: LifecycleDecisionRow
messages.description:
LifecycleDecisionRow is one row of the lifecycle decision log
(AC-2(d)/(e)/(f)). Derived from the signed audit buffer; `event_id` is
the jti from the JWS payload.

messages.name: RecertificationStatusRow
messages.description:
RecertificationStatusRow is one row of the per-account recertification
posture (AC-2(j)). `last_decision` values: "approved" | "revoked" |
"deferred" | "" (never reviewed).

messages.name: RoleBindingRow
messages.description: RoleBindingRow is one row of the role-binding inventory (AC-2(j)/(k)).

services.name: AC2EvidenceService
services.description:
AC2EvidenceService produces NIST SP 800-53 AC-2 evidence reports for an
admin's tenant scope. All 5 RPCs are tenant-scoped (admin session
interceptor) and emit a self-audit event (identity.ac2_evidence.read) on
every call.

Reports are computed on demand from the live aoid.* tables + aoid.audit_buffer.
`as_of` enables point-in-time evidence (uses created_at <= as_of clause).

methods.name: GetAccountInventory
methods.description:
GetAccountInventory returns the account inventory for the tenant
(AC-2(a)/(b) evidence). Optional `kind_filter` narrows to human / service
/ workload / admin.

methods.name: GetRoleBindings
methods.description:
GetRoleBindings returns the role-binding inventory for the tenant
(AC-2(j)/(k) evidence).

methods.name: GetRecertificationStatus
methods.description:
GetRecertificationStatus returns the recertification posture per account
(AC-2(j) evidence): when last reviewed, by whom, next due, days overdue.

methods.name: GetDormantAccounts
methods.description:
GetDormantAccounts returns accounts past their dormancy threshold
(AC-2(3) evidence). `dormancy_threshold_days` overrides the tenant
policy threshold for ad-hoc reports.

methods.name: GetLifecycleDecisions
methods.description:
GetLifecycleDecisions returns the audit-derived lifecycle decision log
(AC-2(d)/(e)/(f) evidence). Filters by action prefix + time range.

fields.name: remaining
fields.description: remaining lists the caller's IdP links AFTER the unlink succeeds.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: AccountSelfServiceUpdateMyProfileRequest
messages.description: PATCH semantics: only set (present) fields are updated.

fields.name: key_prefix
fields.description: First 12 chars only — safe to display. Full secret is never returned.

messages.name: CommunicationPrefs
messages.description:
CommunicationPrefs. security_alerts is documented as "always-on by policy" —
the backend ignores attempts to set false (returns OK but persists true).
product_updates + marketing honor the user's setting.

messages.name: IdPLink
messages.description:
IdPLink is one external-IdP login method linked to the caller's global
identity (Obj 14). idp_subject + idp_email are the values the IdP asserted
at link time (recorded on the link row, never promoted to the identity).
created_at is an RFC3339 string — the link rows are global (no per-tenant
timestamptz coercion the portal would need to localize differently).

fields.name: kind
fields.description: "totp" | "webauthn" | "piv"

fields.name: label
fields.description: User-supplied label (e.g., "iPhone YubiKey")

fields.name: is_last_factor
fields.description:
Server-computed UX hint: if true, removing this factor would leave the user
with zero MFA factors. The portal disables the "Remove" button when this is
true and the tenant requires MFA. Service-layer enforcement is the source
of truth (TRD 08-03 — MGMT-02).

fields.name: tenant_display_name
fields.description:
Resolved display name from aoid.tenants.display_name — avoids the portal
needing a separate GetMyTenantSummary RPC.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: UNION of scopes across non-revoked refresh_tokens for this (account, client) pair.
fields.label: repeated

services.name: AccountSelfService
services.description:
AccountSelfService provides end-user self-service surfaces for an authenticated
session subject. All RPCs derive the actor's account_id + tenant_id from the
session context (set by the AOID Connect interceptor stack — TRD 08-04); no
RPC accepts account_id or tenant_id as an input field. The portal (TRD 08-06+)
is the canonical client.

MFA enrollment continues to use AuthnService (Obj 3, TRD 03-05). MFA REMOVAL
is here because the last-factor policy gate (MGMT-02) is service-layer logic
that doesn't naturally belong on AuthnService. Session listing + revoke is on
EndUserSessionService (Obj 3, TRD 03-05) and is NOT duplicated here.

methods.name: GetMyProfile
methods.description:
MGMT-01 — Profile management. Both Get and Update return MyProfile (the
canonical projection of the caller's account). Lint exceptions disable
RPC_RESPONSE_STANDARD_NAME / RPC_REQUEST_RESPONSE_UNIQUE for these two
because UpdateMyProfile is semantically an upsert that returns the same
shape as Get; introducing wrapper Response messages would force the
portal (Dart) to unwrap on every call for no contract benefit.
buf:lint:ignore RPC_RESPONSE_STANDARD_NAME
buf:lint:ignore RPC_REQUEST_RESPONSE_UNIQUE

methods.name: UpdateMyProfile
methods.description:
buf:lint:ignore RPC_RESPONSE_STANDARD_NAME
buf:lint:ignore RPC_REQUEST_RESPONSE_UNIQUE

methods.name: ListMyMFAFactors
methods.description: MGMT-02 — MFA factor listing + removal (with last-factor policy gate)

methods.name: ListMyOAuthGrants
methods.description: MGMT-04 — OAuth grant listing + revoke (one row per (account, client) pair)

methods.name: ListMyApiKeys
methods.description: MGMT-04 — API key listing + self-revoke

methods.name: ListMyIdPLinks
methods.description:
MGMT-05 — Linked IdP listing + self-unlink (Obj 14 GID-08). The caller's
identity is resolved from the session; UnlinkMyIdP refuses to remove the
last remaining link (CodeFailedPrecondition) to prevent login lockout.

enums.name: BackendHealthState
enums.description:
BackendHealthState mirrors the gobreaker v2 circuit breaker states.
CLOSED = healthy, in rotation. OPEN = unhealthy, not in rotation.
HALF_OPEN = recovering, limited probe requests allowed.

messages.name: BackendEntry
messages.description:
BackendEntry is one upstream backend's runtime health snapshot,
reported by GetBackendHealth.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Upstream backend URL, e.g. "https://backend-a:8443".

fields.name: weight
fields.description: Configured SWRR weight for this backend.

fields.name: health_state
fields.description: Current circuit breaker state.

fields.name: consecutive_failures
fields.description: Number of consecutive probe failures in the current window.

fields.name: last_probe_time
fields.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp of the last health probe attempt.
Empty string if no probe has been run yet.

fields.name: hostname_filter
fields.description: Optional: filter to a specific hostname. Empty = return all backends.

fields.name: backends
fields.description: All backends across all routes (or filtered by hostname_filter).
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: snapshot_time
fields.description: Timestamp when this snapshot was taken (RFC 3339).

fields.name: version
fields.description: Semantic version of this AOEdge binary, e.g. "1.0.0".

fields.name: commit
fields.description: Git commit SHA the binary was built from.

fields.name: built_at
fields.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the build.

fields.name: go_version
fields.description: Go toolchain version, e.g. "go1.26.1 (FIPS)".

fields.name: fips_module
fields.description: Identifier of the FIPS-validated cryptographic module in use.

fields.name: eden_proto_schema_version
fields.description:
Eden proto schema version (or commit) this binary was generated
against. Lets operators detect drift between the AOEdge binary's
expected contracts and what AOID / AOCore / AOAudit are running.

fields.name: alive
fields.description: Liveness: process is up and able to serve admin RPCs.

fields.name: ready
fields.description:
Readiness: data plane is ready to accept external traffic (TLS
listener bound, upstream connection pool healthy, policy bundle
loaded if Objective 10 has shipped).

fields.name: fips_mode
fields.description:
FIPS mode flag — true when the binary was built with BoringCrypto
and the runtime has verified the FIPS module is the active crypto
provider. Required to be true for FedRAMP Moderate / High / IL4 /
IL5 deployments.

fields.name: status_detail
fields.description:
Human-readable status detail, e.g. "ready", "draining",
"upstream_unreachable", "policy_bundle_stale".

fields.name: routes
fields.description: Routes in the live route table, one entry per (hostname, path_pattern) pair.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: total_routes
fields.description: Total number of routes in the current table.

messages.name: RouteEntry
messages.description:
RouteEntry is one (hostname, path_pattern) route, reported by ListRoutes.
Carries only observable operational data; Tenant / Identity / Policy
fields are intentionally absent (those are Obj 9 / Obj 10 concerns).

fields.name: hostname
fields.description: The hostname this route is keyed on (e.g., "api.example.com").

fields.name: path_pattern
fields.description: The path pattern this route matches (e.g., "/v2/*", "/api/users").

fields.name: backend_count
fields.description: Number of backends configured for this route.

fields.name: healthy_backend_count
fields.description: Number of backends currently healthy (circuit breaker CLOSED).

services.name: AOEdgeAdminService
services.description:
AOEdgeAdminService exposes the headless boundary proxy's operational
surface: health, FIPS mode reporting, and build/version metadata.
The data plane (TLS termination, WAF, DDoS, geo, DLP, identity proxy,
policy evaluation) is NOT exposed here — those are handled at the
TCP/HTTP listener, not via Connect RPC. This service is reachable
only from the platform's internal admin network over mTLS.

AOEdge owns: SC-7 boundary protection, identity-aware proxy, WAF,
DDoS, geo, egress DLP. AOEdge does NOT own: credential issuance
(AOID), AI-aware policy (AOCore), audit storage (AOAudit).

methods.name: HealthCheck
methods.description:
HealthCheck reports liveness, readiness, and FIPS posture for a
single AOEdge instance. Used by L4 LBs for backend selection and
by operators verifying FIPS mode is active in regulated
deployments.

methods.name: GetBuildInfo
methods.description:
GetBuildInfo returns the binary's build metadata: version,
commit, build timestamp, Go version, FIPS module identifier, and
the eden proto schema version this binary was generated against.
Used for compliance evidence (which build is enforcing the
boundary right now) and for cross-fleet drift detection.

methods.name: ListRoutes
methods.description:
ListRoutes returns the current active route table as seen by this instance.
Used by operators and dashboards to inspect which hostnames and paths AOEdge
is currently routing, and the health status of each route's backends.
This reflects the live atomic.Pointer[RouteTable] — always consistent.

methods.name: GetBackendHealth
methods.description:
GetBackendHealth returns the health state of all registered upstream backends.
Exposes the circuit breaker state machine so operators can observe which backends
are in rotation without needing direct access to the data plane.

messages.name: AuditBatch
messages.description:
AuditBatch wraps a set of events with signing metadata.
Emitted as a single OTLP log record once per dispatcher batch; raw event
bytes travel as their own OTLP log records and are referenced by the
merkle_root signature.

NOTE: AuditBatch.event_count is int32; bumping to int64 is deferred to
Obj 11 (operational-posture objective). A 2^31 event-count batch is far
beyond v1 dispatch sizing.

fields.name: batch_id
fields.description: rs/xid

fields.name: merkle_root
fields.description: hex SHA-256 root of batch leaf hashes

fields.name: root_signature
fields.description: Ed25519 or KMS signature over merkle_root (hex)

fields.name: signing_key_id
fields.description: key identifier (local or KMS ARN)

messages.name: BundleReloadEvent
messages.description:
BundleReloadEvent — emitted by the signed-bundle reloader on EVERY reload
attempt (AUTHZ-06): a successful atomic-swap (applied), a rejected bad
signature, a Rego compile failure, or a Git pull failure. This is the
audit record of the policy-plane control loop (SC6).

PII-safety: this message MUST NOT carry the signing private key, the
verification public key bytes, or the bundle file contents. Only the
commit_sha + signer_identity (operator string from the signature's trusted
comment) + bundle_digest (the hex digest that WAS verified). A compromised
bundle must not be able to log/assert its own trust root.

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: may be "" — system event, not request-scoped

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: may be "" — deployment-wide single bundle in Obj 10 (per-tenant bundles are Obj 11)

fields.name: commit_sha
fields.description: Git HEAD SHA of the pulled bundle

fields.name: previous_commit_sha
fields.description: the SHA that was active before this reload (for rollback correlation)

fields.name: signer_identity
fields.description: operator/key identity from the detached-signature trusted comment; never the key bytes

fields.name: bundle_digest
fields.description: hex of the deterministic bundle digest that crypto/ed25519.Verify ran against

fields.name: outcome
fields.description: "applied" | "rejected_signature" | "compile_error" | "pull_failed"

fields.name: outcome_detail
fields.description: operator-readable detail (e.g. "rego parse error in admin.rego:14"); never raw input/key

fields.name: rego_module_count
fields.description: number of .rego modules compiled into the prepared query

fields.name: reload_latency_ms
fields.description: pull + verify + compile + swap wallclock

messages.name: ConnectionLog
messages.description:
ConnectionLog is emitted once per request (allowed or blocked).
schema_version: increment when adding non-optional fields.

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: always 1 for v1; bump on breaking change

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: rs/xid (20 chars)

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: W3C traceparent trace-id (hex)

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: W3C traceparent span-id (hex)

fields.name: identity
fields.description: "anonymous" or actor_id

fields.name: decision
fields.description: "allow" | "block" | "error"

fields.name: block_reason
fields.description: empty when decision=allow

fields.name: chain_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hex of (prev_chain_hash || canonical(this event))

messages.name: DDoSEvent
messages.description: DDoSEvent is emitted on every rate-limit or slow-loris rejection.

fields.name: limit_type
fields.description: "l4_conn" | "l7_ip" | "l7_identity" | "l7_tenant" | "slow_loris" | "smuggling"

fields.name: source_identifier
fields.description: IP | identity | tenant

messages.name: DLPFinding
messages.description:
DLPFinding is emitted when DLP scanning detects a pattern match.

HARD INVARIANT (PII-safety): this message and its DLPMatch entries
MUST NEVER store the matched payload bytes. Only the pattern_id,
operator-readable pattern_name, and byte offset+length are recorded.
A schema-lint test enforces this contract — see the top-of-file note
for the prohibited field names.

fields.name: mode
fields.description: "block" | "redact" | "audit"

messages.name: DLPMatch
messages.description:
DLPMatch carries a single pattern hit inside a DLPFinding. NO PII payload
bytes — only pattern identifiers and byte coordinates (offset+length).

messages.name: GeoDecision
messages.description: GeoDecision is emitted on every geo enforcement evaluation.

fields.name: decision
fields.description: "allow" | "block"

messages.name: IdentityMintEvent
messages.description:
IdentityMintEvent — emitted by the AOEdge IAP middleware on every
successful X-AOEdge-Identity-Context JWT mint (IAP-04). One event per
request that crosses to upstream with a freshly-signed identity context.

PII-safety: the signed JWT itself is NOT included — it travels in the
X-AOEdge-Identity-Context request header. The audit stream logs only
metadata (sub, tnt, aal, kid, tok_ref, entitlements_count). The
schema-lint test forbids signed_jwt | jwt | id_token | access_token
field names on this message.

fields.name: kid
fields.description: AOEdge active signing key id

fields.name: sub
fields.description: AOID account UUID from validated credential

fields.name: tnt
fields.description: tenant SLUG (NOT UUID — see Obj 9 RESEARCH §6 pitfall)

fields.name: tok_ref
fields.description: bound to validated credential (16 chars hex)

fields.name: entitlements_count
fields.description: count only — entitlement strings not logged for size

fields.name: ttl_seconds
fields.description: identity-context exp - iat at mint time

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant UUID (resolved from tnt slug); for audit-stream filtering

messages.name: IdentityValidationEvent
messages.description:
IdentityValidationEvent — emitted by the AOEdge IAP middleware on every
credential validation: JWT (IAP-01), opaque token via RFC 7662
introspection (IAP-02), API key (IAP-03), or anonymous-route admission
(IAP-07). BOTH accept and reject paths emit one event.

PII-safety: this message MUST NOT carry the raw credential bytes. Only
`tok_ref` (first-8-byte SHA-256 hex of the raw credential, 16 chars) is
included — same pattern as the AOID identity-context tok_ref. The
schema-lint test in aoedge_audit_lint_test.go asserts the forbidden
field names raw_token | raw_key | password | plaintext | authorization
and bare token | secret are absent file-wide.

fields.name: cred_type
fields.description: "jwt" | "opaque" | "api_key" | "anonymous"

fields.name: tok_ref
fields.description: hex(sha256(raw_credential))[:8] => 16 chars; "" for anonymous

fields.name: outcome
fields.description: "accepted" | "rejected_invalid" | "rejected_expired" | "rejected_wrong_audience" | "rejected_wrong_issuer" | "rejected_aoid_unreachable" | "rejected_inactive"

fields.name: outcome_reason
fields.description: operator-readable detail; never includes credential bytes

fields.name: iss
fields.description: expected issuer (configured AOID URL); "" when cred_type=api_key/anonymous

fields.name: sub
fields.description: accepted: AOID account UUID; rejected: ""

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant UUID resolved by hostname routing (Obj 3); always populated for non-anonymous

fields.name: aal
fields.description: resolved AAL ("AAL1"/"AAL2"/"AAL3"); "" on reject

fields.name: latency_ms
fields.description: wallclock validation latency incl. any AOID round-trip

fields.name: cache_hit
fields.description: true when validation served from in-process introspection/API-key cache

fields.name: route_id
fields.description: chi.RouteContext.RoutePattern() at validation time

messages.name: MatchedRuleEntry
messages.description:
MatchedRuleEntry describes a single Coraza rule match inside a WAFEvent.
The fragment_b64 field carries an operator-bounded fragment for incident
triage; v1 is base64-encoded and capped at ~256 bytes by the producer.
DLPFinding / DLPMatch must NOT include any analogue of this field —
see the PII-safety invariant at the top of this file.

fields.name: fragment_b64
fields.description: base64 (waf only); capped at producer

messages.name: PolicyDecisionEvent
messages.description:
PolicyDecisionEvent — emitted by the authz middleware on EVERY policy
evaluation: allow, deny, or engine error (AUTHZ-01). Mirrors the way Geo
emits GeoDecision unconditionally. The allow/deny ratio per route is
derived from these events + the aoedge_authz_* Prometheus counters.

PII-safety: this message MUST NOT carry the raw policy input document, the
raw identity-claims blob, or any credential. The full input is
reconstructible by correlating on request_id with the IdentityValidationEvent
(Obj 9) + GeoDecision (Obj 8) events. Schema-lint asserts policy_input /
raw_input / token / secret / etc. are absent file-wide.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant UUID resolved by hostname routing (Obj 3); credential-authoritative tenant for cross-tenant decisions

fields.name: route_id
fields.description: chi.RouteContext.RoutePattern() at evaluation time

fields.name: sub
fields.description: identity subject (account UUID from Obj 9 iap.Identity); "" for anonymous

fields.name: decision
fields.description: "allow" | "deny" | "error"

fields.name: deny_reason
fields.description: "wrong_tenant" | "missing_entitlement" | "outside_business_hours" | "geo_blocked" | "risk_exceeded" | "engine_error" | ""; operator-readable, never raw input

fields.name: policy_query
fields.description: the Rego rule path queried for this route (e.g. "data.aoedge.authz.decision")

fields.name: backend_group
fields.description: split-horizon output (AUTHZ-04); "" when not identity-routed

fields.name: required_entitlement
fields.description: the entitlement the route required (AUTHZ-02); "" when none

fields.name: require_stepup
fields.description: policy output: higher AAL required (AUTHZ-05 step-up interplay)

fields.name: required_aal
fields.description: "AAL2"|"AAL3" when require_stepup=true; "" otherwise

fields.name: bundle_revision
fields.description: active bundle commit SHA at evaluation time (correlates to BundleReloadEvent)

fields.name: latency_ms
fields.description: policy eval wallclock latency (microsecond budget; reported in ms)

fields.name: fail_open
fields.description: true when decision=allow was produced by AOEDGE_AUTHZ_FAIL_OPEN on an engine error

messages.name: StepUpChallengeEvent
messages.description:
StepUpChallengeEvent — emitted by the AOEdge IAP middleware when per-route
policy requires a higher AAL than the validated credential carries
(IAP-06). The middleware issues either a browser 302 to AOID
/authorize?acr_values=... or a non-browser 403 with RFC 9470
WWW-Authenticate: Bearer error="insufficient_user_authentication".

PII-safety: redirect_uri_host carries only scheme+host+path of the AOID
authorize endpoint — query parameters (state, nonce, redirect_uri) are
NOT logged. The schema-lint test forbids the bare `redirect_uri` field.

fields.name: required_aal
fields.description: "AAL2" | "AAL3"

fields.name: current_aal
fields.description: AAL from validated credential ("AAL1" typical)

fields.name: channel
fields.description: "browser" (302) | "api" (403)

fields.name: redirect_uri_host
fields.description: scheme://host/path of AOID /authorize; NO query string

fields.name: sub
fields.description: current account UUID (already authenticated at lower AAL)

messages.name: WAFEvent
messages.description: WAFEvent is emitted on every WAF rule trigger.

fields.name: tx_id
fields.description: Coraza transaction ID

fields.name: action
fields.description: "block" | "would_block" | "pass"

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description:
Owning tenant UUID. AOEdge knows which tenant the request is for
(from upstream OAuth context) and supplies it here; the AOID
server uses it as the lookup parameter on the (tenant_id,
key_prefix) index.

fields.name: raw_key
fields.description: The raw API key under validation. Format: "aoid_<env>_<52-char-base32>".

fields.name: valid
fields.description: True only when the key passes every check.

fields.name: api_key_id
fields.description: Only set when valid=true.

fields.name: account_id
fields.description: Only set when valid=true.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: Echoes the request tenant_id for caller-side consistency check.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: Only set when valid=true.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: reason
fields.description:
Coarse reason when valid=false. One of:
  "unknown"        — prefix not found OR revoked OR hash mismatch
  "expired"        — prefix found, hash matched, but past expires_at
  "invalid_format" — raw_key doesn't start with "aoid_" or is too short
Empty when valid=true.

services.name: ApiKeyValidationService
services.description:
ApiKeyValidationService is the AOEdge-facing API-key validation
surface per VAL-03. It is a SEPARATE service from
CredentialAdminService because the Connect interceptor stack differs:

  - CredentialAdminService: mTLS + adminauth + tenant-scope + audit
  - ApiKeyValidationService: mTLS + validation_api_consumers
    allow-list + audit

Calls without mTLS or with an unknown mTLS peer-cert SPIFFE ID are
rejected by the interceptor BEFORE reaching the handler (per AOID's
success criterion #5 of Objective 5 + VAL-03 spec). The handler
itself enforces a defense-in-depth allow-list re-check.

Wire shape is INTENTIONALLY narrow — only the raw_key and tenant_id
cross the boundary. The response distinguishes valid keys from
invalid ones with a coarse reason string (forward-compat: future
reasons can be added without breaking wire compat — adopters parse
on known string set + treat unknown as "invalid").

methods.name: ValidateApiKey
methods.description:
ValidateApiKey validates a raw API key. Response.valid is true only
when the key is well-formed, not revoked, not expired, and its
stored hash matches the submitted raw key under
platform/auth/secrethasher.Verify. The response does NOT leak
revocation state — revoked keys return reason="unknown".

messages.name: IngestBreakGlassEventRequest
messages.description:
IngestBreakGlassEventRequest carries the contents of one JSONL line
from the aoidemergency replay file. The server uses these fields
verbatim (it does NOT recompute or override original_jti /
original_issued_at) — this is by design so the AOAudit timeline
reconstructs the outage window faithfully.

fields.name: original_jti
fields.description:
original_jti is the UUID minted at issuance time. Must be uuid-shaped.
Server dedups on this field: re-replay is a no-op.

fields.name: original_issued_at
fields.description:
original_issued_at is the timestamp the CLI recorded at JWT issuance.
Server SETS the audit row's emitted_at to this value (NOT
request-arrival time). Server rejects values older than 30 days
(sanity bound — older replays are suspicious).

fields.name: subject
fields.description:
subject is the JWT 'sub' claim — the principal the break-glass
credential authenticated.

fields.name: tenant
fields.description:
tenant is the tenant slug the credential was scoped to (or '*' for
chassis-wide recovery operations).

fields.name: scope
fields.description: scope is the manifest scope: 'admin.recovery' | 'service.recovery'.

fields.name: reason
fields.description:
reason is the operator-provided human-readable reason from the
authorization manifest (≥ 20 chars enforced at issuance time).

fields.name: operator
fields.description:
operator is the requested_by email from the manifest — the operator
who drafted the request (NOT the ≥ 2 signers; see manifest_digest_b64
to recover the full signer set offline).

fields.name: manifest_digest_b64
fields.description:
manifest_digest_b64 is the base64-std-encoded SHA-256 digest of the
raw manifest bytes the CLI consumed. The replay tool can produce
the manifest file alongside the replay log; downstream observers
can recompute and confirm the manifest wasn't substituted post-hoc.

fields.name: accepted
fields.description:
accepted is true if the event landed (or was idempotently de-duped).
false (with a non-OK status) indicates a hard rejection.

fields.name: event_id
fields.description:
event_id is the server-side UUID assigned to the audit row. Stable
across replays — re-replay returns the original event_id.

services.name: AuditService
services.description:
AuditService provides audit log queries + a break-glass replay endpoint
for the aoidemergency tool (AOID Obj 11 TRD 11-03).

methods.name: IngestBreakGlassEvent
methods.description:
IngestBreakGlassEvent receives a previously-issued break-glass
credential event from the aoidemergency `replay` subcommand AFTER
recovery completes. The server preserves the ORIGINAL issuance
timestamp (from the request body) when inserting into the local
audit buffer — incident-response timeline reconstruction depends on
this fidelity. Idempotent by original_jti: replays of the same JSONL
file return accepted=true without inserting duplicate rows.

ACL (enforced by the host service interceptor chain): admin role
required. Tenant-admin actors MAY replay events scoped to their
tenant (original operator may not retain super-admin credentials
post-recovery); super-admin actors may replay any tenant's events.

Write-capability gating: this RPC is classified WRITE by the AOID
write-capability interceptor (Obj 11 TRD 11-01), so replay against a
replica region returns Unavailable — operators must direct replay
at the post-recovery primary.

messages.name: AuditEvent
messages.description:
AuditEvent is the admin-facing projection of a single audit-buffer row.
`jws_signature` is the full JWS Compact string for downstream replay +
verify; the raw signed payload bytes are NOT surfaced (the verifier
reconstructs the payload from the typed fields).

messages.name: AuditQueryServiceGetAuditEventRequest
messages.description: AuditQueryServiceGetAuditEventRequest fetches one event by jti.

messages.name: AuditQueryServiceGetAuditEventResponse
messages.description: AuditQueryServiceGetAuditEventResponse wraps the requested event.

messages.name: AuditQueryServiceQueryAuditEventsRequest
messages.description:
AuditQueryServiceQueryAuditEventsRequest filters the local audit buffer.
All filters are AND-combined. Empty filters match everything.

fields.name: action_prefix
fields.description:
Match any action with one of these prefixes, e.g. "auth." or
"identity.account.".
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: decision
fields.description: "allow" | "deny" | "partial".
fields.label: optional

messages.name: AuditQueryServiceQueryAuditEventsResponse
messages.description:
AuditQueryServiceQueryAuditEventsResponse returns a page of events plus the
earliest timestamp still in the local buffer.

fields.name: retention_horizon
fields.description:
Earliest emitted_at the local buffer still retains. Events older than
this require AOAudit-side lookup.

services.name: AuditQueryService
services.description:
AuditQueryService surfaces tenant-scoped audit events to admins (MGMT-06).
Reads from the local aoid.audit_buffer; events older than the buffer
retention window MUST be queried via AOAudit directly (this service returns
what's available locally and includes a `retention_horizon` hint).

Every successful call emits a self-audit event (identity.audit_query.read).

methods.name: QueryAuditEvents
methods.description:
QueryAuditEvents returns a page of audit events matching the supplied
filter. All filter fields are optional except tenant_id.

methods.name: GetAuditEvent
methods.description: GetAuditEvent returns a single audit event by event_id (jti).

messages.name: AuthData
messages.description:
AuthData is the shared authentication payload returned by SignUp,
Login, and RefreshToken. Wrapped in per-RPC response types per
buf STANDARD lint convention (RPC_REQUEST_RESPONSE_UNIQUE).

messages.name: InitiateSocialLoginRequest
messages.description:
InitiateSocialLoginRequest starts a consumer social-login flow. provider is
one of "google"|"apple"|"microsoft"|"facebook"|"x". redirect_uri is where the
user is sent after auth (Flutter deep-link or web origin) — it MUST match the
server's redirect allowlist.

messages.name: InitiateSocialLoginResponse
messages.description:
InitiateSocialLoginResponse carries the provider authorization URL the client
must open, plus the opaque state JWT for client-side correlation. Tokens are
NOT returned here — the HTTP callback delivers them via redirect.

services.name: AuthService
services.description: AuthService handles authentication operations.

methods.name: InitiateSocialLogin
methods.description:
InitiateSocialLogin starts a consumer social-login flow (Google, Apple,
Microsoft, Facebook, X). User-scoped — NO company_id. Tokens are delivered
out-of-band by the GET /auth/social/callback HTTP handler via redirect.

messages.name: AuthnServiceLogoutRequest
messages.description:
AuthnServiceLogoutRequest is service-prefixed to avoid collision with
auth.proto's LogoutRequest in the same package.

messages.name: AuthnServiceLogoutResponse
messages.description:
AuthnServiceLogoutResponse is service-prefixed to avoid collision with
auth.proto's LogoutResponse in the same package.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: No email — passwordless username-less flow.

fields.name: stepup_token
fields.description: continuation token from the original error

fields.name: available_methods
fields.description: "webauthn", "piv"
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: options_json
fields.description: CredentialCreationOptions as raw JSON (browser-direct).

fields.name: backup_codes
fields.description: plaintext — shown ONCE, never re-fetchable
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: kind
fields.description: "password", "totp", "webauthn", "piv"

fields.name: provisioning_uri
fields.description: otpauth://... — client QR-renders

fields.name: secret
fields.description: raw base32 (for manual entry)

fields.name: piv
fields.description: PIV requires the mTLS handshake — no inline assertion bytes needed

fields.name: new_aal
fields.description:
Server side-effect: marks stepup_token as consumed; the original RPC
is re-callable by the client with normal session credentials.

fields.name: attestation_response_json
fields.description: CredentialCreationResponse from browser (raw JSON).

messages.name: LoginNextStep
messages.description: LoginNextStep tells the client what authentication step is required next.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: "mfa_required" | "session_established" | "stepup_required"

fields.name: available_methods
fields.description: "totp", "webauthn", "backup_code"
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: current_aal
fields.description: "AAL1", "AAL2", "AAL3"

fields.name: required_aal
fields.description: for stepup

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description:
No additional fields — UPN extracted from mTLS client cert at the
transport layer by TRD 03-07's PIV interceptor.

fields.name: code
fields.description:
Implicit: requires PasswordLoginStart() to have set two_factor_user_id
(cookie session-bound).

messages.name: PasswordLoginCompleteRequest
messages.description:
No body — session-bound. Returned only when no MFA is required AND
policy permits password-only login (rare; AAL1 only).

fields.name: token_prefix
fields.description: matched via prefix; current session cannot self-revoke

fields.name: token_prefix
fields.description: first 8 chars of session token for display

services.name: AuthnService
services.description:
AuthnService provides end-user authentication primitives — password,
TOTP, WebAuthn (registration + login + passwordless discoverable login),
PIV/CAC mTLS login, credential management, and step-up assurance flows.

Tenant scope: login-initiating RPCs (PasswordLoginStart, LoginWith*)
require tenant_id in the request body because no session exists yet.
Once a session is established, tenant is read from the session cookie
by the Connect interceptor and is NOT accepted as a body field.

Step-up: when a downstream service decides the current AAL is
insufficient, it returns a CodePermissionDenied error with a "stepup_token"
header. The client calls BeginStepUp(stepup_token), completes the
challenge, calls FinishStepUp, then retries the original RPC.

methods.name: PasswordLoginStart
methods.description: === Password flow ===

methods.name: EnrollTOTP
methods.description: === TOTP flow ===

methods.name: BeginWebAuthnRegistration
methods.description: === WebAuthn flow ===

methods.name: LoginWithPIV
methods.description:
=== PIV/CAC flow ===
The actual mTLS handshake happens at the transport layer; this RPC
tells the server "I'm here on mTLS — please establish my session."

methods.name: BeginStepUp
methods.description: === Step-up flow ===

methods.name: Logout
methods.description:
=== Logout + credential mgmt ===
Logout uses service-prefixed message names to avoid collision with the
existing auth.proto's LogoutRequest/LogoutResponse in the same package.

services.name: EndUserSessionService
services.description: EndUserSessionService provides introspection over the current session.

services.name: BridgeService
services.description: BridgeService handles platform event bridge operations.

messages.name: CompanyData
messages.description:
CompanyData is the shared company payload returned by Create/Get/Update
(wrapped in per-RPC responses) and contained in List/GetAncestors/
GetDescendants list responses. Per buf STANDARD lint convention
(RPC_REQUEST_RESPONSE_UNIQUE).

fields.name: company_type
fields.description: holding, subsidiary, brand, standalone

services.name: CompanyService
services.description: CompanyService manages company hierarchy.

messages.name: ApiKey
messages.description:
ApiKey is the canonical wire shape for an API key record. NO raw_key
field — that material is carried ONLY by MintApiKeyResponse.raw_key
and RotateApiKeyResponse.raw_key, each emitted EXACTLY ONCE.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal database UUID (primary key).

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: Owning tenant UUID.

fields.name: owner_account_id
fields.description: Owner account UUID (the account this key acts on behalf of).

fields.name: key_prefix
fields.description:
First 12 chars of the raw key (e.g. "aoid_prod_AB"). Indexed for
O(1) lookup at validation time; safe to log + display.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
Human-friendly label supplied at mint time. 1..255 chars; no
control chars (server strips them via strings.Map at mint time).

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: Allowed OAuth-shaped scopes (e.g. "read", "admin:tenant").
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: issued_at
fields.description: Issuance timestamp.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Optional expiry. Absent = never expires.

fields.name: last_used_at
fields.description:
Last successful validation timestamp. Updated cheaply on every
ValidateApiKey hit; race-y is acceptable (audit hint, not a
security boundary).

fields.name: revoked_at
fields.description: Revocation timestamp. Absent = active.

fields.name: revoked_reason
fields.description: Human-readable revocation reason. Empty when revoked_at is absent.

messages.name: CertificateSummary
messages.description:
CertificateSummary is the wire shape for a single cert row. The
underlying X.509 DER is returned verbatim in cert_der; PEM is
generated on the wire for caller convenience but never persisted
as such (DER is the storage canon).

fields.name: cert_id
fields.description: UUID

fields.name: owner_account_id
fields.description: empty for workload

fields.name: status
fields.description: 'active' | 'revoked' | 'expired'

fields.name: owner_account_id
fields.description: optional filter

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: optional

fields.name: raw_key
fields.description: RAW key material. SHOWN EXACTLY ONCE.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: human-readable; recorded on the row

fields.name: revoked
fields.description: Revoked predecessor record.

fields.name: api_key
fields.description: Newly minted replacement record.

fields.name: raw_key
fields.description: RAW replacement key. SHOWN EXACTLY ONCE.

messages.name: IssueCertificateRequest
messages.description:
IssueCertificateRequest carries the admin-controlled inputs for
IssueCertificate. CSR is PEM-encoded (PKCS#10); the server parses to
DER, verifies the self-signature, then applies any override fields
before signing.

fields.name: owner_account_id
fields.description: optional; empty for workload certs

fields.name: csr_pem
fields.description: PEM-encoded CSR (PKCS#10)

fields.name: subject_override
fields.description: optional RFC 2253 DN; overrides CSR

fields.name: validity_days
fields.description: optional; server clamps to per-kind max

fields.name: kind
fields.description: 'client' | 'server' | 'workload'

fields.name: cert_pem
fields.description: convenience PEM-encoded form

fields.name: cert_id
fields.description: UUID; previous cert (for audit cross-reference)

fields.name: reason
fields.description: RFC 5280 §5.3.1 reason code 0-10

services.name: CredentialAdminService
services.description:
CredentialAdminService manages non-OAuth credentials issued by AOID:

  - API keys (TRD 05-05): tenant-scoped opaque bearer tokens for
    server-to-server authentication. Prefix-lookup + Argon2id/PBKDF2
    verification.
  - mTLS client + workload certificates (TRD 05-06): leaf X.509 certs
    signed by AOID's intermediate CA (root resides in HSM, intermediate
    wrapped via KMS:Encrypt). CSR-based issuance with admin override
    for SAN/Subject. Renewal is policy-neutral (does NOT auto-revoke
    old cert). Revocation atomically flips status + appends to
    aoid.revocations table + notifies the CRL refresh loop.
  - SPIFFE workload SVIDs (TRD 05-07): short-lived workload certs
    issued directly by pubkey (no CSR), 30-day max TTL.

All RPCs share the same Connect handler so admin tooling reaches one
canonical surface for tenant credential lifecycle. Tenant scoping +
audit emission match the AccountAdminService / OAuthAdminService
precedent: every request carries tenant_id as field 1; super admins
may target any tenant; mutations emit platform/audit.Event with the
action constants defined in TRD 05-05 / 05-06 / 05-07.

NOTE (cross-TRD coordination): This file is APPEND-ONLY. TRD 05-05
(apikey) and TRD 05-07 (SVID) extend this service with additional
RPCs; the contract is "first writer creates the file, later writers
append within the same `service CredentialAdminService { ... }`
block."

---------- mTLS client + workload certificates (CRED-06) ----------

Implemented by TRD 05-06 — see /Users/justin/dev/aoid/internal/pki/.

methods.name: IssueCertificate
methods.description:
IssueCertificate signs a leaf cert from a caller-supplied CSR.
kind: 'client' | 'server' | 'workload'.
Workload certs MAY also be issued via the dedicated SVID flow
(TRD 05-07) for tighter TTL controls.

methods.name: RenewCertificate
methods.description:
RenewCertificate signs a new cert based on a fresh CSR. Old cert is
NOT auto-revoked; admin should explicitly revoke if needed.

methods.name: RevokeCertificate
methods.description:
RevokeCertificate marks the cert revoked and adds the serial to the
revocation table; CRL refresh + OCSP responder pick up immediately.

methods.name: GetCertificate
methods.description: GetCertificate returns metadata for one cert.

methods.name: ListCertificates
methods.description: ListCertificates returns certs in a tenant.

methods.name: MintApiKey
methods.description:
MintApiKey creates a new API key. The raw key is returned EXACTLY
ONCE in the response and is never persisted in plaintext. The
server generates a 32-byte CSPRNG suffix, encodes as Crockford
base32, prepends "aoid_<env>_", hashes via
platform/auth/secrethasher, and stores the prefix + hash.

methods.name: ListApiKeys
methods.description:
ListApiKeys returns API keys in the given tenant (admin scope) or
optionally narrows to a single owner.

methods.name: GetApiKey
methods.description:
GetApiKey returns metadata for one API key (NOT the raw key — that
is only available in the MintApiKey / RotateApiKey responses).

methods.name: RevokeApiKey
methods.description:
RevokeApiKey marks an API key as revoked. Subsequent validation
calls for the same prefix return reason="unknown" (we do not leak
revocation state to validation callers).

methods.name: RotateApiKey
methods.description:
RotateApiKey is a convenience composition: revoke the supplied key
and mint a fresh one in the same tenant + owner, preserving the
scopes + name. The new raw_key appears EXACTLY ONCE in the response.

messages.name: AttributeMapping
messages.description:
AttributeMapping describes how to extract AOID account fields from an
upstream IdP's claims / SAML attributes. Paths are dotted JSON-path-lite
(e.g. "email", "user.email", "profile.name"). Matches the shape consumed
by Eden oidcrp.ClaimMap (TRD 06-02) and the SAML attribute extractor
(TRD 06-01).

fields.name: id
fields.description: id is the mapping UUID.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant_id is the owning tenant UUID.

fields.name: name
fields.description: name is the admin-facing label.

fields.name: email_path
fields.description: email_path locates the user's email in the upstream payload.

fields.name: email_verified_path
fields.description:
email_verified_path locates the boolean "email verified" signal.
Empty means AOID treats the upstream email as verified.

fields.name: sub_path
fields.description: sub_path locates the upstream stable subject identifier. Required.

fields.name: preferred_username_path
fields.description: preferred_username_path locates the preferred username (optional).

fields.name: name_path
fields.description: name_path locates the display name (optional).

fields.name: groups_path
fields.description: groups_path locates a list-typed groups claim/attribute (optional).

fields.name: assurance_level_path
fields.description:
assurance_level_path locates an upstream-asserted assurance level
(e.g. acr, amr; optional). When present, the value passes through
the federation policy's assurance_level_override gate.

fields.name: custom_attrs
fields.description:
custom_attrs maps AOID-side attribute names → upstream paths for
tenant-specific extensions (e.g. {"clearance": "extensions.clearance"}).
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: created_at is when the mapping was created.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: updated_at is the last mutation timestamp.

messages.name: DownstreamSP
messages.description:
DownstreamSP is a SAML Service Provider that AOID acts as IdP for
(outbound federation, TRD 06-09). For OIDC RPs, AOID uses
OAuthAdminService.CreateClient from Obj 4 — DownstreamSP is the
SAML-specific equivalent.

fields.name: id
fields.description: id is the DownstreamSP UUID.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant_id is the owning tenant UUID.

fields.name: name
fields.description: name is the admin-facing label.

fields.name: entity_id
fields.description: entity_id is the SP entityID (SAML EntityDescriptor/@entityID).

fields.name: acs_url
fields.description:
acs_url is the SP's Assertion Consumer Service URL where AOID
POSTs the SAML Response.

fields.name: slo_url
fields.description:
slo_url is the SP's Single Logout Service URL. Empty if SLO is not
wired for this SP.

fields.name: signing_cert_pem
fields.description:
signing_cert_pem is the SP's signing certificate, used to verify
SAML AuthnRequests when the SP signs them. Empty when the SP does
not sign AuthnRequests.

fields.name: nameid_format
fields.description:
nameid_format is the SAML NameID format AOID emits in assertions.
Valid values: "email" | "persistent" | "transient".

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: created_at is when the SP was first registered.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: updated_at is the last mutation timestamp.

messages.name: ExternalIdP
messages.description:
ExternalIdP is an upstream identity provider (SAML IdP or OIDC OP) that
a tenant has configured to federate into AOID. Inbound federation
(TRD 06-07 / 06-08) consumes these records.

fields.name: id
fields.description: id is the ExternalIdP UUID (database primary key).

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant_id is the owning tenant UUID.

fields.name: name
fields.description: name is the admin-facing display label.

fields.name: kind
fields.description:
kind is the federation protocol. Valid values:
  "saml" — uses saml_metadata_xml.
  "oidc" — uses oidc_issuer_url + oidc_client_id + oidc_client_secret.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: enabled toggles the IdP without deleting the configuration.

fields.name: saml_metadata_xml
fields.description:
saml_metadata_xml is the verbatim SAML 2.0 metadata document.
Populated only when kind == "saml". MUST be validated by
platform/saml.XSWGuard + ParseAndCacheMetadata (TRD 06-01) before
AOID persists. AOID encrypts at rest (TRD 06-05).

fields.name: oidc_issuer_url
fields.description:
oidc_issuer_url is the OIDC issuer (iss claim) URL.
Populated only when kind == "oidc".

fields.name: oidc_client_id
fields.description:
oidc_client_id is AOID's registered client_id at the upstream OP.
Populated only when kind == "oidc".

fields.name: oidc_client_secret_set
fields.description:
oidc_client_secret_set is a write-only signal. On responses it
reports whether a secret is configured. The plaintext is never
returned on read paths (Get / List). Use the dedicated rotate flow
in FederationAdminService to change a secret.

fields.name: oidc_scopes
fields.description:
oidc_scopes are the scopes AOID requests on each authorization
(e.g. ["openid", "profile", "email"]). Populated only when
kind == "oidc".
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: attribute_mapping_id
fields.description:
attribute_mapping_id binds this IdP to an AttributeMapping (below)
that translates upstream claims/attributes into AOID account fields.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: created_at is when the IdP record was first persisted.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: updated_at is the last mutation timestamp.

messages.name: FederationPolicy
messages.description:
FederationPolicy gates federation behavior per (tenant, external IdP).
Stored one-per-tenant-per-IdP. Primary key is the (tenant_id, idp_id) pair.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant_id is the owning tenant UUID.

fields.name: idp_id
fields.description: idp_id is the ExternalIdP UUID this policy applies to.

fields.name: allowed
fields.description:
allowed gates the IdP entirely. When false, federation attempts are
rejected regardless of other settings.

fields.name: jit_enabled
fields.description:
jit_enabled allows just-in-time provisioning of new accounts on first
successful federation. When false, the account must pre-exist.

fields.name: jit_default_role_id
fields.description:
jit_default_role_id is the role assigned to a JIT-provisioned account.
Empty means no automatic role assignment. UUID string when present.

fields.name: assurance_level_override
fields.description:
assurance_level_override sets the AAL recorded on the session
regardless of the upstream assertion. Empty means "use upstream value
when present, else fall back to AAL1". Valid values: "AAL1" | "AAL2" | "AAL3".

fields.name: required_attributes
fields.description:
required_attributes lists AOID-side attribute names that MUST be
resolvable from the upstream assertion via the AttributeMapping.
Federation attempts missing a required attribute are rejected.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: email_domain_allowlist
fields.description:
email_domain_allowlist limits federated accounts to email addresses
matching one of the listed domains. Empty means "no domain filter".
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: created_at is when the policy was first persisted.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: updated_at is the last mutation timestamp.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description:
One-time plaintext client_secret. Empty for public clients
(token_endpoint_auth_method == "none") and for private_key_jwt
clients (which authenticate via signed JWT assertions).

fields.name: limit
fields.description:
Pagination — same shape as Obj 2 ListAccounts / ListGroups.
0 means server default (100). Server caps at 500.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Opaque, server-issued cursor. Empty for first page.

fields.name: next_cursor
fields.description: Empty == no more results.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description:
New plaintext client_secret. One-time emission — the server retains
only the bcrypt hash.

fields.name: client_name
fields.description: Empty string means no change.

fields.name: allowed_scopes
fields.description:
Replacement list. Empty means no change. To clear, callers must use
a server-side admin tool — proto cannot distinguish unset vs. empty.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: auto_consent
fields.description:
auto_consent always applies — the field is a bool, so callers must
send the desired value on every UpdateClient call. To make an
"unchanged" semantics possible, AOID treats UpdateClient as a
full-replace of the addressable fields documented here.

messages.name: OAuthClient
messages.description:
OAuthClient is the canonical client registration data. NO client_secret
is carried here — secret material appears only in CreateClientResponse
and RotateClientSecretResponse, never read back via GetClient.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal database UUID (primary key).

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: Owning tenant UUID.

fields.name: client_id
fields.description:
Public OAuth client identifier. Typically a generated UUID-without-dashes
or a URL-safe random string (~22 chars). DIFFERENT from id.

fields.name: client_name
fields.description: Human-friendly label shown in admin UIs and consent screens.

fields.name: client_type
fields.description:
"public" | "confidential". Confidential clients require a token-endpoint
auth method other than "none"; public clients require PKCE.

fields.name: grant_types
fields.description:
Allowed OAuth 2.1 grant flows. Valid values:
  "authorization_code", "client_credentials",
  "device_code", "refresh_token".
A single client may hold multiple grants (e.g. authorization_code +
refresh_token). The server validates each token request's grant_type
against this list.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: redirect_uris
fields.description:
Allowlisted redirect URIs. OAuth 2.1 requires exact-match comparison —
the server validates HTTPS scheme and exact equality on each
authorize request. Format checking is NOT enforced in proto.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: allowed_scopes
fields.description:
Allowlisted OAuth scopes. The server intersects this list against
the scopes requested on each authorize / token call.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: token_endpoint_auth_method
fields.description:
"client_secret_basic" | "private_key_jwt" | "none".
"none" is valid only for public clients (PKCE-based). The server
rejects "none" for confidential clients.

fields.name: jwks_uri
fields.description:
JWKS endpoint URL. Only meaningful when
token_endpoint_auth_method == "private_key_jwt"; empty otherwise.
The server validates this consistency at create/update time and
fetches JWKs on demand to verify client assertions.

fields.name: auto_consent
fields.description:
First-party clients (auto_consent=true) skip the user consent screen.
Reserved for clients owned by the operating tenant.

services.name: OAuthAdminService
services.description:
OAuthAdminService manages OAuth 2.1 client registrations on AOID.
Bound under super_admin or tenant_admin mTLS CNs (Obj 2 adminauth resolver).
All RPCs scope to tenant_id; super_admin can target any tenant.

Client secrets are emitted exactly once — on CreateClient and
RotateClientSecret. They are NEVER read back via GetClient or any
other RPC; the server persists them bcrypt-hashed (cost 12) per
the Obj 3 password-hashing precedent.

Tenant scoping (enforced by the same TenantScopeInterceptor used by
AccountAdminService):
  - Every request carries tenant_id as field 1.
  - Super admins may target any tenant.
  - Tenant admins are rejected at the request boundary when tenant_id
    does not match their bound tenant.

Audit emission: every successful mutation emits a platform/audit.Event
with the action constants added in TRD 04-05 (oauth.client.*).

methods.name: CreateClient
methods.description:
CreateClient registers a new OAuth client and returns the canonical
record plus the one-time plaintext client_secret (empty for public
clients and for private_key_jwt clients).

methods.name: GetClient
methods.description:
GetClient fetches a single client by its public client_id within the
tenant. The response never includes client_secret material.

methods.name: UpdateClient
methods.description:
UpdateClient mutates non-collection attributes (client_name,
allowed_scopes, auto_consent). redirect_uris and grant_types mutate
through the dedicated Add/Remove RPCs to preserve per-mutation audit
trails.

methods.name: DeleteClient
methods.description: DeleteClient permanently removes a client registration.

methods.name: ListClients
methods.description: ListClients returns a paginated list of clients in the tenant.

methods.name: RotateClientSecret
methods.description:
RotateClientSecret generates a new client_secret, persists the bcrypt
hash, and returns the plaintext exactly once.

methods.name: AddRedirectURI
methods.description: AddRedirectURI appends a redirect_uri to the client's allowlist.

methods.name: RemoveRedirectURI
methods.description: RemoveRedirectURI removes a redirect_uri from the client's allowlist.

messages.name: ClientIdPOption
messages.description:
ClientIdPOption is the (OAuth client, external IdP) pairing that controls
whether a given IdP is offered in a client's /authorize IdP picker.
RemoveClientIdPOption returns this with enabled = false (disable, not
delete) to preserve the admin audit trail.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.description: tenant_id is the owning tenant (canonical field 1 for tenant scope).

fields.name: name
fields.description: name is the admin-facing display label.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: kind is "saml" or "oidc".

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: enabled toggles the IdP at creation time (default false).

fields.name: saml_metadata_xml
fields.description:
saml_metadata_xml is required when kind == "saml". MUST validate via
platform/saml.XSWGuard + ParseAndCacheMetadata (TRD 06-01).

fields.name: oidc_issuer_url
fields.description: oidc_issuer_url is required when kind == "oidc".

fields.name: oidc_client_id
fields.description: oidc_client_id is required when kind == "oidc".

fields.name: oidc_client_secret
fields.description:
oidc_client_secret is the plaintext upstream client secret. Required
when kind == "oidc". Stored bcrypt-hashed at rest (TRD 06-05).
Never returned on read paths.

fields.name: oidc_scopes
fields.description:
oidc_scopes overrides the default ["openid", "profile", "email"]
when kind == "oidc".
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: attribute_mapping_id
fields.description:
attribute_mapping_id binds the IdP to a previously-created
AttributeMapping. The RPC returns CodeFailedPrecondition if the
mapping does not exist in the tenant.

fields.name: idp
fields.description:
idp carries the persisted record. oidc_client_secret is never set
on responses; oidc_client_secret_set reflects the configured state.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: limit defaults to server-side 100, capped at 500.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: cursor is opaque, server-issued. Empty for first page.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: kind optionally filters to "saml" or "oidc" only.

fields.name: next_cursor
fields.description: next_cursor is empty when there are no more results.

fields.name: client_name
fields.description:
client_name is the admin-facing label (forwarded to
OAuthAdminService.CreateClient).

fields.name: redirect_uris
fields.description:
redirect_uris is the SP's redirect_uri allowlist. At least one
entry required.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: allowed_scopes
fields.description:
allowed_scopes overrides the federation defaults
["openid", "profile", "email"]. Empty list means "use defaults".
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: client
fields.description:
client is the canonical OAuthClient record returned by
OAuthAdminService.CreateClient.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description:
client_secret is the one-time plaintext secret emitted exactly
once at creation. The server retains only the bcrypt hash.

fields.name: entity_id
fields.description: entity_id is the SP entityID (SAML EntityDescriptor/@entityID).

fields.name: acs_url
fields.description: acs_url is the SP's Assertion Consumer Service URL.

fields.name: slo_url
fields.description:
slo_url is the SP's Single Logout Service URL. Empty if SLO is
not wired.

fields.name: signing_cert_pem
fields.description:
signing_cert_pem is the SP's signing certificate (PEM-encoded),
used to verify SAML AuthnRequests when the SP signs them.
Empty bytes when the SP does not sign AuthnRequests.

fields.name: nameid_format
fields.description: nameid_format is "email" | "persistent" | "transient".

fields.name: name
fields.description: name replaces the label. Empty means no change.

fields.name: email_path
fields.description:
Path fields below replace the stored path when non-empty; empty
means no change. To clear a path (e.g. drop email_verified), use
the literal sentinel "-" — the application layer translates "-" to
"no path / clear". This sentinel avoids proto's inability to
distinguish unset string from empty string.

fields.name: custom_attrs
fields.description:
custom_attrs is a full-replace when non-empty. Empty map means
no change. To clear, callers must use a dedicated tool.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: name
fields.description: name replaces the display label. Empty means no change.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description:
enabled always applies (the field is a bool — callers must send the
intended value on every UpdateExternalIdP call).

fields.name: saml_metadata_xml
fields.description:
saml_metadata_xml replaces the stored metadata when kind == "saml".
Empty bytes mean no change. Replacement bytes MUST re-validate via
platform/saml.XSWGuard + ParseAndCacheMetadata.

fields.name: oidc_issuer_url
fields.description:
oidc_issuer_url replaces the issuer URL when kind == "oidc".
Empty means no change.

fields.name: oidc_client_id
fields.description:
oidc_client_id replaces the client_id when kind == "oidc".
Empty means no change.

fields.name: oidc_client_secret
fields.description:
oidc_client_secret replaces the stored client secret when set.
Empty means no change — the existing secret is retained.

fields.name: oidc_scopes
fields.description:
oidc_scopes replaces the requested scopes when kind == "oidc".
Empty list means no change.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: attribute_mapping_id
fields.description:
attribute_mapping_id replaces the mapping binding. Empty means
no change.

fields.name: jit_default_role_id
fields.description:
jit_default_role_id is the role assigned to JIT-provisioned accounts.
Empty means no automatic role assignment.

fields.name: assurance_level_override
fields.description:
assurance_level_override sets the AAL recorded on federated sessions.
Empty means "use upstream value when present, else AAL1". Valid
values: "AAL1" | "AAL2" | "AAL3".

fields.name: required_attributes
fields.description:
required_attributes lists AOID-side attribute names that MUST be
resolvable from the upstream assertion. Federation attempts missing
a required attribute are rejected.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: email_domain_allowlist
fields.description:
email_domain_allowlist limits federated accounts to email addresses
matching one of the listed domains. Empty means "no domain filter".
fields.label: repeated

services.name: FederationAdminService
services.description:
FederationAdminService manages AOID's federation surface (Obj 6):
inbound external IdPs (SAML / OIDC), attribute mappings, per-tenant
federation policy, and outbound SAML downstream Service Providers.
OIDC RPs (outbound) are managed via Obj 4's OAuthAdminService;
RegisterDownstreamClient here is a thin convenience wrapper that
fans out to OAuthAdminService.CreateClient with federation-friendly
defaults (response_types code, grant_types authorization_code +
refresh_token, allowed_scopes "openid profile email").

All RPCs are mTLS-only and bound under super_admin or tenant_admin
CNs by Obj 2's adminauth resolver and TenantScopeInterceptor:
  - Every request carries tenant_id as field 1.
  - Super admins may target any tenant.
  - Tenant admins are rejected when tenant_id does not match their
    bound tenant.

Audit emission: every successful mutation emits a platform/audit.Event
with the action constants added in TRD 06-03 (federation.*).

Pagination: list RPCs use the (limit, cursor) shape that matches
Obj 4's OAuthAdminService.ListClients (limit default 100, max 500;
opaque server-issued cursor; empty next_cursor means "no more results").

----- ExternalIdP -----

methods.name: CreateExternalIdP
methods.description:
CreateExternalIdP registers a new upstream IdP for the tenant.
For OIDC IdPs, oidc_client_secret is consumed and stored
bcrypt-hashed (TRD 06-05) and never returned. For SAML IdPs,
saml_metadata_xml MUST validate via platform/saml.XSWGuard +
ParseAndCacheMetadata (TRD 06-01) or the RPC returns
CodeInvalidArgument.

methods.name: GetExternalIdP
UpdateExternalIdP
methods.description:
UpdateExternalIdP mutates IdP attributes. Setting
oidc_client_secret to a non-empty value replaces the stored secret;
empty means "leave unchanged". To clear an enabled IdP, set
enabled = false explicitly.

methods.name: DeleteExternalIdP
methods.description:
DeleteExternalIdP permanently removes an IdP configuration along
with the attached FederationPolicy. Federated accounts retain their
local records; subsequent federation attempts via the deleted IdP
are rejected.

methods.name: ListExternalIdPs
methods.description: ListExternalIdPs returns a paginated list of IdPs in the tenant.

methods.name: CreateAttributeMapping
methods.description:
CreateAttributeMapping registers a new attribute mapping in the
tenant. Multiple ExternalIdPs may reference the same mapping.

methods.name: UpdateAttributeMapping
methods.description:
UpdateAttributeMapping mutates the mapping. Existing IdPs referencing
this mapping pick up the change on their next federation attempt.

methods.name: DeleteAttributeMapping
methods.description:
DeleteAttributeMapping removes the mapping. The RPC returns
CodeFailedPrecondition if any ExternalIdP still references it.

methods.name: ListAttributeMappings
methods.description:
ListAttributeMappings returns a paginated list of mappings in the
tenant.

methods.name: UpsertFederationPolicy
methods.description:
UpsertFederationPolicy creates or replaces the federation policy for
a (tenant, IdP) pair. Primary key is the (tenant_id, idp_id) pair —
there is at most one policy per IdP per tenant.

methods.name: GetFederationPolicy
methods.description: GetFederationPolicy fetches the policy for a (tenant, IdP) pair.

methods.name: DeleteFederationPolicy
methods.description:
DeleteFederationPolicy removes the policy. With no policy present,
federation attempts default to the safe denial path.

methods.name: RegisterDownstreamSP
methods.description:
RegisterDownstreamSP creates a new SAML SP registration that AOID
will issue assertions to.

methods.name: ListDownstreamSPs
methods.description: ListDownstreamSPs returns a paginated list of SPs in the tenant.

methods.name: DeleteDownstreamSP
methods.description:
DeleteDownstreamSP removes an SP registration. In-flight assertions
already issued remain valid for their lifetime.

methods.name: RegisterDownstreamClient
methods.description:
RegisterDownstreamClient is a thin convenience wrapper around
OAuthAdminService.CreateClient (Obj 4). It applies federation-friendly
defaults (client_type=confidential, grant_types=
[authorization_code, refresh_token], token_endpoint_auth_method=
client_secret_basic, default scopes openid+profile+email) and
returns the canonical OAuthClient + one-time client_secret. Admin
UIs that need finer control should call OAuthAdminService.CreateClient
directly instead.

methods.name: AddClientIdPOption
methods.description:
AddClientIdPOption enables an (OAuth client, external IdP) pairing so the
IdP is offered in that client's /authorize IdP picker. Idempotent on the
(client_id, idp_id) pair. The response carries the resulting option with
enabled = true.

methods.name: RemoveClientIdPOption
methods.description:
RemoveClientIdPOption DISABLES (enabled = false — it does NOT delete the
row, preserving the admin audit trail) an (OAuth client, external IdP)
pairing. The response carries the option with enabled = false.

messages.name: CreateTenantRequest
messages.description:
CreateTenantRequest creates a new tenant. Super-admin only. No tenant_id
field — this RPC creates the tenant.

AOID Obj 10 TRD 10-05 extension:
  - intended_kms_key_id        (field 5) — KMS key URI for token signing
  - intended_audit_kms_key_id  (field 6) — KMS key URI for audit signing

Both KMS URI fields are REQUIRED when isolation_tier ∈
{'cryptographic','physical'}; empty when isolation_tier='logical'.
Operators pre-provision the KMS keys (AWS GovCloud KMS, Azure Gov
Managed HSM, on-prem HSM); AOID consumes the URIs as opaque secrets.
Adding fields 5+6 is wire-compatible with logical-tier callers that
omit them.

fields.name: isolation_tier
fields.description: isolation_tier defaults to "logical" when empty.

fields.name: intended_kms_key_id
fields.description:
Required iff isolation_tier in ('cryptographic','physical').
KMS key URI (awskms://..., azkeys://..., pkcs11://...) for token signing.
Opaque to AOID; never logged plaintext.

fields.name: intended_audit_kms_key_id
fields.description:
Required iff isolation_tier in ('cryptographic','physical').
KMS key URI for audit-event signing (separate trust domain from tokens).

services.name: AccountAdminService
services.description:
AccountAdminService is the admin surface for AOID identity lifecycle:
accounts, groups, roles, entitlements, and tenants. All RPCs are mTLS-only
and require an authenticated admin actor (super_admin or tenant_admin).

Tenant scoping (enforced by server.NewTenantScopeInterceptor):
  - Every mutating request carries tenant_id as field 1.
  - Super admins may target any tenant (including uuid.Nil for tenant ops).
  - Tenant admins are rejected at the request boundary when tenant_id
    does not match their bound tenant.

Audit emission: every successful mutation emits a platform/audit.Event
with the action constants added in TRD 02-03 (identity.*).

See AOID .planning/objectives/02-account-model-admin-lifecycle/ for the
design backing this contract.

-------------------------- Accounts (LIFE-01/02/03/05/08, MGMT-08) -----

methods.name: ProvisionAccount
methods.description: ProvisionAccount creates a human user account.

methods.name: ProvisionServiceAccount
methods.description:
ProvisionServiceAccount creates a service_account or workload_identity
with an optional or required expires_at lifetime.

methods.name: GetAccount
methods.description: GetAccount retrieves a single account by id within the tenant.

methods.name: ListAccounts
methods.description: ListAccounts returns a paginated list of accounts in the tenant.

methods.name: UpdateAccount
methods.description:
UpdateAccount mutates non-lifecycle attributes (display_name, email,
org_affiliation, clearance). Status transitions go through
Suspend/Recover/Deprovision.

methods.name: SuspendAccount
methods.description: SuspendAccount transitions the account to "suspended". Attributes retained.

methods.name: RecoverAccount
methods.description: RecoverAccount transitions a suspended account back to "active".

methods.name: DeprovisionAccount
methods.description: DeprovisionAccount transitions the account to "deleted" (soft delete).

methods.name: DefineGroup
methods.description: DefineGroup creates a tenant-scoped group.

methods.name: ListGroups
methods.description: ListGroups returns groups in the tenant.

methods.name: AddAccountToGroup

messages.name: SelfRecoveryServiceConsumeRecoveryTokenRequest
messages.description:
SelfRecoveryServiceConsumeRecoveryTokenRequest validates the URL-safe
64-char hex token from the email link + sets the new password. The server
validates new_password per NIST SP 800-63B (TRD 03-N policy).

messages.name: SelfRecoveryServiceConsumeRecoveryTokenResponse
messages.description:
SelfRecoveryServiceConsumeRecoveryTokenResponse returns success + a
one-time login session cookie value the portal sets to log the user in
immediately after recovery. login_session_token is empty on failure.

messages.name: SelfRecoveryServiceRequestRecoveryRequest
messages.description:
SelfRecoveryServiceRequestRecoveryRequest carries the tenant slug + the
email entered by the user. Email enumeration defense: the server returns
success regardless of whether the account exists.

messages.name: SelfRecoveryServiceRequestRecoveryResponse
messages.description:
SelfRecoveryServiceRequestRecoveryResponse is intentionally empty for v1.
Reserved for future rate-limit hints.

services.name: SelfRecoveryService
services.description:
SelfRecoveryService is the public (unauthenticated) self-service account
recovery surface. Two endpoints:

 1. RequestRecovery — initiates an email-verified recovery flow. Returns
    success even for unknown accounts (defense against email enumeration —
    see AOID runbook §H.3).
 2. ConsumeRecoveryToken — validates the token + sets a new password,
    then returns a one-time login session token so the portal can log the
    user in immediately.

LIFE-04. Single-use tokens, default 30-minute TTL. Per-tenant policy
configurable. The mTLS / admin-session interceptor stack is NOT applied
to this service; it's reachable by anonymous traffic from the Obj 8 portal.

methods.name: RequestRecovery
methods.description: RequestRecovery starts the recovery flow. Always returns success.

methods.name: ConsumeRecoveryToken
methods.description: ConsumeRecoveryToken validates the token + sets a new password.
