fields.name: requested_path
fields.description:
Workload path component of the target SPIFFE ID. MUST start with
/sa/ (service account) or /workload/. Rejected when empty, /, or
beginning with /admin/.

fields.name: csr_pem
fields.description:
EC P-256 CSR PEM (BEGIN/END CERTIFICATE REQUEST). The CA verifies
the self-signature before binding the public key.

fields.name: requested_ttl_seconds
fields.description:
Optional caller-suggested TTL in seconds. Server clamps to
[1, 86400]. 0 = use spiffe.DefaultSVIDTTL (1h).

fields.name: svid_id
fields.description: Persistent UUID for the SVID row (used by RevokeSVID).

fields.name: spiffe_id
fields.description: Canonical spiffe://<trust-domain>/<path> URI.

fields.name: serial
fields.description: Decimal serial-number string (matches aoid.svids.serial_number).

fields.name: cert_pem
fields.description: Leaf cert in PEM form.

fields.name: cert_der
fields.description: Leaf cert in DER form (saves callers a parse step).

fields.name: issued_at
fields.description: Effective NotBefore.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Effective NotAfter (post-clamp).

fields.name: page_size
fields.description: Optional page size. Defaults to 100, capped at 500 server-side.

fields.name: page_token
fields.description: Opaque continuation token; empty = first page.

fields.name: svids
fields.description: Page of SVIDs, newest first.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: next_page_token
fields.description: Opaque token to fetch the next page; empty when done.

fields.name: svid_id
fields.description: UUID of the SVID row to revoke.

fields.name: svid
fields.description: Returns the updated row so the caller can observe revoked_at.

messages.name: SVIDSummary
messages.description: SVIDSummary is the wire view of one aoid.svids row.

fields.name: revoked_at
fields.description: Absent = active; present = revoked.

services.name: SvidService
services.description:
SvidService issues SPIFFE X.509-SVIDs to workloads already attested via
mTLS. This is a SEPARATE service from CredentialAdminService because the
interceptor stack differs:

  - CredentialAdminService: mTLS + adminauth + tenant-scope
  - SvidService:            mTLS + spiffe-id-extraction (no admin RBAC)

The caller's CURRENT valid mTLS cert (which carries a spiffe:// URI SAN)
IS the attestation in v1. The handler trusts the Connect interceptor to
have validated the chain + extracted the SPIFFE ID into the request
context. A revoked SVID would fail the mTLS handshake before reaching
this handler.

Re-attestation flow: a workload re-issues its SVID by re-handshaking
(mTLS) with its CURRENT still-valid SVID; the renewal request uses the
OLD SVID as the attestation cert. If the old SVID is revoked or
expired, the mTLS handshake fails BEFORE the IssueSVID handler runs --
the handler never sees the request. This is by design and means the
handler does not implement an attestation re-check.

methods.name: IssueSVID
methods.description:
IssueSVID mints a short-lived (<=24h) X.509-SVID for the caller's
requested workload path within the chassis trust domain. The path
MUST start with /sa/ or /workload/ (admin/system paths rejected).

methods.name: ListMySVIDs
methods.description:
ListMySVIDs returns SVIDs issued to the caller's underlying mTLS cert.
Lets a workload audit which credentials were minted from its
current attestation. Scoped by caller's mTLS cert serial (extracted
by the interceptor) -- callers may not enumerate other workloads'
SVIDs.

methods.name: RevokeSVID
methods.description:
RevokeSVID marks a previously-issued SVID revoked. Append-only on
the revocations table; the CRL refresh loop picks up the change
within one refresh interval.

messages.name: WebhookData
messages.description:

(RPC_REQUEST_RESPONSE_UNIQUE).

services.name: WebhookService
services.description: WebhookService manages webhook subscriptions and deliveries.

Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint64 instead.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int32s.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int64s.
Always four bytes. More efficient than uint32 if values are often greater than 2^28.

A string must always contain UTF-8 encoded or 7-bit ASCII text.
